title: Array-of-Queues push_back() method doesn't work as expected, observed by checking size() or printing the queue itself.
body:
Can you please attach an example that shows the issue or missing feature?  (Must be openly licensed, completely self-contained so can directly run what you provide. Ideally use test_regress format, see https://veripool.org/guide/latest/contributing.html?highlight=test_regress#reporting-bugs)

   I have an example small self containted tesbench tb.sv for this, will put onto a fork. Appears to work correctly on Vivado XSim, ModelsimASE, QuestaFSE, Riviera; Verilator was the only simulator I have that showed this problem.

What output from that test indicates it is wrong, and what is the correct or expected output?  (Or, please make test self-checking if possible.)

   Used an assert to check that queue size() is correct.

What 'verilator' command line do we use to run your example?

   /usr/local/bin/verilator --binary -Wno-CASEINCOMPLETE -Wno-REALCVT -Wno-SELRANGE -Wno-TIMESCALEMOD -Wno-UNSIGNED -Wno-WIDTH --timing --assert --autoflush -sv -CFLAGS -O1 -j 21 -top tb -o sim.exe +define+SIMULATION tb.sv


What 'verilator --version' are you using?  Did you try it with the git master version?  Did you try it with other simulators?

    Noticed it on 5.043 (`5.043 devel rev v5.042-5-g0eaa9ed14`) and 5.044.

What OS and distribution are you using?

    Ubuntu22.04

May we assist you in trying to fix this in Verilator yourself?

    I'll do some minimal digging and provide the tb.sv.
